questions.question: Which task is performed by a book-keeper? A analysing accounting ratios, B interpreting the income statement, C posting entries from the journals to the ledgers, D preparing a statement of financial position

Which statement is correct? A Accounting involves measuring profits and losses, B Accounting is the recording of financial transactions, C Book-keeping involves communicating financial data, D Book-keeping requires the use of ratio analysis
In which book of prime entry does a trader record cash discount received? A cash book, B general journal, C purchases journal, D sales journal

The analysis columns of a petty cash book show the following totals: postage $20, stationery $31. How should these totals be posted to the ledger? A credit bank account with $51, B credit postage account with $20 and stationery account with $31, C debit cash account with $51, D debit postage account with $20 and stationery account with $31
Which business documents are not entered in the books of prime entry? 1 credit note, 2 debit note, 3 invoice, 4 statement of account. A 1 and 3, B 1 only, C 2, 3 and 4, D 2 and 4 only

A trader has the following ledger account: heat and light account. What does the balance on 1 January, year 2 represent? A heat and light outstanding, B heat and light refunded, C overpayment of heat and light, D prepayment of heat and light

Which statement about a trial balance is correct? A It is a list of the ledger balances on a particular date, B It is part of the double entry system of book-keeping, C It is proof that all items have been posted to the correct accounts, D It is proof that all transactions have been entered in the books of account

Trevor prepared his financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2020. He then discovered that he had omitted to record a cash payment, $240, for insurance for the 12 months ending 30 November 2021. What was the effect of this error on his statement of financial position? A capital $220 overstated, other receivables $20 understated, cash $240 overstated, B capital $20 overstated, other receivables $220 understated, cash $240 overstated, C capital $220 understated, other receivables $20 overstated, cash $240 understated, D capital $20 understated, other receivables $220 overstated, cash $240 understated

A payment of $68 for insurance has been entered in the insurance account as $86. Which double entry will correct this? A debit suspense $18, credit insurance $18, B debit suspense $36, credit insurance $36, C debit insurance $18, credit suspense $18, D debit insurance $36, credit suspense $36

A business has received a bank statement. The bank statement shows the following entries: 1 a cheque issued to a credit supplier, 2 a cheque received from a credit customer, 3 a monthly rent payment made by standing order, 4 charges made by the bank. Which items appear in the debit column of the bank statement? A 1, 3 and 4, B 1 and 3 only, C 1 only, D 2

On 31 May the bank statement of a business showed a positive bank balance of $1790. Comparing the bank statement with the cash book the following items were discovered: The bank had charged $35 in error, Unpresented cheques totalled $550, Uncredited deposits totalled $250. What was the cash book balance? A $1455, B $1490, C $1525, D $2125

What would not be included in a sales ledger control account? A cash sales recorded in the cash book, B cheques received from credit customers recorded in the cash book, C goods sold on credit recorded in the sales journal, D irrecoverable debts written off recorded in the journal

Nadia provided the following information for the month of July: 1 July sales ledger control account balance debit $1750, credit $215. For the month of July: total of sales journal $5500, total of sales returns journal $360, cheques received from credit customers $4840, irrecoverable debt written off $280, interest charged on credit account $5. There was no credit balance on the account on 1 August. What was the debit balance brought down on the sales ledger control account on 1 August? A $1550, B $1560, C $1980, D $1990

Samir runs a car hire business. He received cash payments for car hire. He also received the proceeds from the sale of an old vehicle. Which type of receipts were they? A car hire capital, proceeds from sale of an old vehicle capital, B car hire revenue, proceeds from sale of an old vehicle capital, C car hire capital, proceeds from sale of an old vehicle revenue, D car hire revenue, proceeds from sale of an old vehicle revenue

The following ledger account appeared in the books of a trader: Rent receivable account. Which entry was made in the income statement for the year ended 31 December 2020 for rent receivable? A $3400 credit, B $3400 debit, C $3800 credit, D $3800 debit

Mandeep depreciates his motor vehicles at the rate of 20% using the straight-line method. A full year's depreciation is provided in the year of purchase. Mandeep bought a motor vehicle on 1 January 2017 for $20000. On 1 June 2020 he bought a second motor vehicle for $10000. What was the depreciation charge on motor vehicles for the year ended 31 December 2020? A $2000, B $4000, C $5000, D $6000

Why does a trader maintain a provision for doubtful debts? 1 to ensure amounts owing to the business for which payment is unlikely to be received are regarded as an expense, 2 to ensure the trade receivables of the business are not overstated, by providing for debts which are unlikely to be paid, 3 to identify credit customers who are not able to settle their accounts by the end of the financial year, 4 to provide for the amounts from credit customers which are unpaid at the end of the financial year. A 1 and 2, B 1, 3 and 4, C 2 and 3 only, D 2, 3 and 4

Why is inventory valued at the lower of cost and net realisable value? A to avoid overstating current assets and sales, B to avoid overstating profit and current assets, C to avoid understating current assets and sales, D to avoid understating profit and current assets

At the end of the financial year Cindy valued her inventory at cost. This valuation incorrectly included damaged goods costing $300. Cindy estimated these goods could be sold for $100. What is the effect of correcting the inventory valuation? A current assets decreases by $200, profit for the year decreases by $200, gross profit decreases by $200, B current assets decreases by $300, profit for the year decreases by $300, gross profit decreases by $300, C current assets increases by $100, profit for the year increases by $100, gross profit increases by $100, D current assets increases by $200, profit for the year increases by $200, gross profit increases by $200
Which business is not a service business? A accountant's practice, B computer repair shop, C flower shop, D hairdressing salon

A trader was preparing her statement of financial position at 30 June 2021. She had four bank loans: 1 $5000 repayable 1 December 2021, 2 $10000 repayable 1 December 2022, 3 $8000 repayable 1 September 2023, 4 $6000 repayable 1 December 2025. What was the total amount of loans that should be recorded as non-current liabilities at 30 June 2021? A $14000, B $15000, C $23000, D $24000

What is shown in a partnership appropriation account? A the capital contributed by each partner, B the distribution of profit/loss among the partners, C the partners' current account balances, D the interest on a partner's loan account

Rajid and Sunil formed a partnership on 1 January 2019 but did not prepare a partnership agreement. They provided the following information: Rajid capital introduced 1 January 2019 $40000, drawings during the year ended 31 December 2019 $5000, share of work 50%. Sunil capital introduced 1 January 2019 $20000, drawings during the year ended 31 December 2019 $3500, share of work 50%. They decided to draw up a partnership agreement for future years. Which item would be most beneficial to Rajid in 2020? A interest on capital, B interest on drawings, C limit on annual drawings, D partnership salaries

A company supplied the following information: issued and paid up share capital $300000, general reserve $45000, long-term bank loan $80000, retained earnings $35000, 6% debentures $25000. What is the total equity? A $335000, B $345000, C $380000, D $413000

Which apply to both debentures and preference shares? A included in capital employed, fixed rate of return, no voting rights, B included in capital employed, fixed rate of return, voting rights, C capital employed, fixed rate of return, voting rights, D capital employed, fixed rate of return, voting rights

The AB Sports Club runs a café for the use of members. The treasurer prepared draft financial statements which showed a deficit of $700 and an accumulated fund of $4600. He then discovered that the closing inventory of the café had been overvalued by $50. How much were the deficit and the accumulated fund after the inventory valuation had been corrected? A deficit $650, accumulated fund $4550, B deficit $650, accumulated fund $4650, C deficit $750, accumulated fund $4550, D deficit $750, accumulated fund $4650

Which accounting statement prepared by the treasurer of a sports club provides a summary of all the monies received and paid out during the financial year? A bank reconciliation statement, B income and expenditure account, C receipts and payments account, D statement of financial position

At the end of the financial year, a manufacturing business owed rent on its factory. The business adjusted its financial statements to account for this. What effects did this adjustment have? A gross profit increase, cost of production decrease, prime cost decrease, B gross profit decrease, cost of production increase, prime cost no effect, C gross profit decrease, cost of production no effect, prime cost no effect, D gross profit decrease, cost of production increase, prime cost increase
What is a direct cost for a manufacturer? A carriage outwards, B depreciation of delivery vehicle, C factory supervisor's salary, D production royalties

A business uses 20% mark up to arrive at its selling prices. During the year it made purchases of $35000 and the inventory decreased from $7000 to $2000. What was the revenue for the year? A $36000, B $40000, C $42000, D $48000
What is needed for the preparation of a statement of affairs? A a trial balance, B an income statement, C books of prime entry, D values of assets and liabilities

A trader provided the following information: cash purchases for the year $146000, credit purchases for the year $108000, trade payables at the year end $14000. What was the trader's trade payables turnover? A 6 days, B 13 days, C 21 days, D 48 days

George provided the following information: non-current assets $15000, inventory $12000, trade receivables $18000, trade payables $8000. His liquid (acid test) ratio was 1.2:1. What was his bank overdraft? A $7000, B $15000, C $17000, D $29500

The following ratios have been calculated for a trader: year 1 profit margin 20%, year 2 profit margin 6%, year 1 return on capital employed (ROCE) 15%, year 2 return on capital employed (ROCE) 9%. What explains these changes? A Drawings have increased by more than profit for the year, B Gross profit has increased but profit for the year has decreased, C Profit for the year has increased and capital has been introduced, D Profit for the year has increased and a long-term loan has been repaid

William is a trader. He has received an order from James to supply goods on credit. When should William regard the revenue as earned? A when William accepts the order from James, B when William delivers the goods to James, C when William receives full payment for the goods from James, D when William receives the first payment on account from James
